messages.text: Near the garden, the little prince heard a soft voice under an apple tree. A fox watched him from the grass.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: Good morning.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Come and play with me. I am lonely.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: I cannot play with you. I am not tame.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: What does tame mean?

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: It means to create ties. To me, you are one boy among many. To you, I am one fox among many.
The word sounded simple, but the fox spoke as if it were a ceremony. Friendship required time, not command.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: Sit a little distance away. I will look at you from the corner of my eye. Each day come a little closer.
The prince obeyed. He learned patient silence. He learned that expectation can make a field of wheat shine like hair in sunlight.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: Come at the same hour. If I know the hour, my heart can prepare itself. That is the use of ceremony.
Their friendship grew gradual and real. When the day came for him to leave, the fox lowered his head.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: You will cry. I have done you no good.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: You have done me good because of the color of wheat. Now go look at the roses again.
He returned to the garden. The roses were beautiful, but they were not unique to him. He had not watered them or protected them.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: You are lovely, but empty. No one could die for you. My rose is responsible to me, and I am responsible to her.
The fox gave his secret before parting. The essential part of a thing cannot be seen by eyes; it becomes visible only through care.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: What is essential is invisible to a careless look. Remember the time you spent on your rose.
The little prince repeated the secret so memory would keep it. His rose was unique because he had loved her with daily attention.
Lonely Earth had not taken his flower from him. It had taught him why she mattered more than a garden.
The fox refused hurry. He treated friendship as a craft, something shaped by repeated presence until ordinary hours gained ceremony.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: Words create mistakes. Silence lets us become patient enough to hear what is changing.
So the prince sat in the grass day after day. The distance between them grew smaller, but the importance of that distance grew clearer.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: On the day I leave, the wheat will remind you of my hair. That seems both happy and sad.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: It is the price of being tame. One accepts tears because the color of wheat becomes a reward.
After hearing the secret, the prince did not feel clever. He felt responsible. Understanding had turned memory into an obligation.
The roses had not changed, but his eyes had. The invisible tie to one flower now mattered more than visible beauty spread across a garden.
The fox never touched him, yet the tame bond became stronger than touch. It lived in hours, footsteps, wheat, and remembered waiting.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: The secret is simple only after you have paid for it with time.
That was why the prince could return to the roses without envy. Care had given him a measure that appearance could not defeat.
The fox did not make friendship easy. He made it exact: same hour, same patience, same willingness to be changed by another life.

messages.name: Fox
messages.text: You become responsible forever, not because anyone forces you, but because your heart learns a name.
The prince carried that sentence back to his rose like a lamp lit for the journey home.
